insights:
The single best deal among current models is MiMo-V2.5, which tops the composite deal score at 816.7. It delivers an intelligence rating of 49 out of 60, tied for the highest on this list, while costing only $0.06 per million tokens. This model also provides a full 1,000K context window and a generation speed of 90 tokens per second, with strong competitive Elo scores of 1431 overall and 1437 on code. The combination of top-tier intelligence, extremely low price, and generous context makes MiMo-V2.5 the clear leader for production workloads where output quality and cost are both critical.

Two notable runners-up are DeepSeek V4 Flash and MiMo-V2-Flash. DeepSeek V4 Flash scores 783.3 with an intelligence of 47, matching MiMo-V2.5’s price and context size but offering a faster 138 tokens per second. The trade-off is a slight dip in intelligence, which may matter for complex reasoning tasks. MiMo-V2-Flash, from February 2026, scores 683.3 with intelligence 41 and a speed of 127 t/s, but its context window is limited to 256K. Step 3.5 Flash is another strong option at the same price, with a lower intelligence of 38 but exceptional speed of 238 t/s, though its context is also capped at 256K. For tasks where speed and very long context are less important than raw capability, the DeepSeek model is a close second.

For the most common use case of cost-sensitive API calls, the actionable recommendation is to use MiMo-V2.5. It provides the best measured intelligence per dollar, and its 1,000K context window handles long documents without expensive chunking. If your application prioritizes throughput and can tolerate slightly lower scores, DeepSeek V4 Flash offers similar pricing with higher speed and the same context. Avoid models with lower intelligence scores even if they are cheaper, such as Nova Micro at $0.03—its intelligence of 10 is inadequate for production reasoning tasks. MiMo-V2.5 therefore balances all key metrics for a wide range of applications.
